category: Subscription-first price masquerading as the default one-time price

consistency.notes: All non-null, non-empty ground-truth labels are supported by the page text or embedded product data. Price/listing fields align with the default 250g variant and one-time purchase selection.

realism.reasons: This reads very plausibly as an html-to-text extraction of a real specialty coffee product page. The structure is highly typical: site nav, product title, subscription/one-time purchase pricing, size selector, stock/cart CTA, tasting notes, brew recommendation, origin details, descriptive copy, related products, embedded variant data, and footer links. The coffee details are coherent and specific in a realistic way: Chelbesa/Danche/Gedeb/Gedeo/Yirgacheffe all fit together geographically; washed process, Ethiopian varieties 74110/74112, and altitude 1,950–2,200 masl are all credible; the tasting notes and brew guidance match a light roasted washed Ethiopia. The harvest window is plausible for Ethiopia. The variant block is also believable: 250g at $24 and 1kg at $82 is sensible specialty pricing, the subscription discount math is correct, and the SKUs/package weights look normal. Voice is concise and commercial without sounding machine-generated. If there is any minor tell, it is only that the text is a bit unusually clean and complete for a raw scrape, but not enough to reduce plausibility.

label.coffee.sensory_text:
Tasting Notes: Jasmine, white peach, Meyer lemon, honey

A bright and fragrant washed Ethiopia from the Danche Washing Station in Chelbesa. We taste jasmine aromatics, white peach sweetness, a clean Meyer lemon sparkle, and a honeyed finish that makes this lot shine on pour-over.
label.coffee.display_tasting_notes: Jasmine, white peach, Meyer lemon, honey
label.coffee.producer_text: Danche Washing Station serves smallholder farmers around Chelbesa in Gedeb, Gedeo. Freshly delivered cherries are depulped, fermented in clean water, washed through grading channels, and dried slowly on raised beds until the parchment reaches export-ready moisture.

coffee.sensory_text: A bright and fragrant washed Ethiopia from the Danche Washing Station in Chelbesa. We taste jasmine aromatics, white peach sweetness, a clean Meyer lemon sparkle, and a honeyed finish that makes this lot shine on pour-over.
coffee.display_tasting_notes: Jasmine, white peach, Meyer lemon, honey
coffee.producer_text: Danche Washing Station serves smallholder farmers around Chelbesa in Gedeb, Gedeo. Freshly delivered cherries are depulped, fermented in clean water, washed through grading channels, and dried slowly on raised beds until the parchment reaches export-ready moisture. This is roasted light for clarity and sweetness. We recommend a 1:16 ratio, soft water, and a flat-bottom dripper to highlight the floral cup.

source_snippets.snippet: Jasmine, white peach, Meyer lemon, honey
